items.title: Gift bags and cruise ship fallout part of Adelaide's COP31 considerations

children.content: A heavily redacted feasibility study into Adelaide's capacity to host the COP31 UN climate summit provides a fleeting glimpse into what was under consideration — including politically fraught floating hotels.

items.title: Hospital cancels birthing services again, forcing expectant parents to travel

children.content: Two regional hospitals, 30 minutes' drive apart, struggle to consistently provide routine medical services, forcing repeated patient diversions.

image.altText: A pregnant woman's belly as she holds hands with someone

items.title: The artworks we can't stop thinking about from the Adelaide Biennial

children.content: The legacy of nuclear testing, environmental degradation and more among the challenges facing Australia, as explored by artists in the Adelaide Biennial of Australian art.

image.altText: Archie Moore, an Indigenous man, mid-50s, in a cap, holds a heart made of gold in his purple-gloved hands.

items.title: More uncounted votes discovered in SA a month after election

children.content: More uncounted votes have been discovered in two electorates a month on from the South Australian state election, and while the state's Electoral Commission says the ballots will not harm the "integrity" of the count, they have not said how many have been found.

articles.title: Missing votes counted in key SA seat see One Nation margin increase

children.content: One Nation has retained the South Australian seat of Narungga, following a count of ballots that were missed on election day.

image.altText: One Nation's Chantelle Thomas at a media conference.

articles.title: Uncovered ballots prompt new count in key SA seat won by One Nation

children.content: The Electoral Commission of South Australia has uncovered more than 600 ballot papers that were not counted in the recent state election, including dozens from the closely contested seat of Narungga on the Yorke Peninsula.

image.altText: A composite image of two women with blonde hair

gallery.altText: A ballot box during an election.

thumbnail.altText: A ballot box during an election.

items.title: SA childcare centre forced to close over seven supervision incidents

children.content: Another Adelaide childcare centre has been forced to shut its doors for three months after seven incidents of children being left unsupervised.

image.altText: Edge Early Learning Centre Munno Para West

items.title: China suddenly grants new beef licences to Australia

children.content: China has granted beef export approval to several new Australian facilities, just months after introducing a range of trade measures to protect its domestic cattle industry.

image.altText: Chef cooking beef with China and Australian flags in the background

items.title: As flight options shrink, this regional city is hoping for a return to rail

children.content: Trains stopped coming to Mount Gambier decades ago, but with fewer flights, locals want them back.

image.altText: A man wearing a red and brown striped jumper stands underneath a black sign with white writing which reads "Mount Gambier"

items.title: 'It's just bulldozed through': Native title holders allege cultural sites have been destroyed by mining company

children.content: A First Nations group on South Australia's Yorke Peninsula says a mining company has bulldozed areas of cultural significance and has started development without a formal agreement with Traditional Owners, but the company says it has "continuously engaged" with the group.

image.altText: Two Aboriginal men in black shirts standing alongside each other with a serious expression to the camera

items.title: Labor's warning over 'timing' of Mount Barker, Murray Bridge commitments

children.content: The Malinauskas government made $300 million in election promises for the cities of Mount Barker and Murray Bridge, but when they will be delivered remains uncertain after the two cities did not elect Labor MPs.

image.altText: A woman wearing red and a man in a suit look at a red sign which reads: Labor's plan for Murray Bridge

items.title: AFL apologises after Butters appeal upheld, umpire abuse conviction quashed

children.content: The AFL appeals board has quashed Zak Butters's conviction for abusive language against umpire Nick Foot, saying a Tribunal member listening in while driving his car for part of the original hearing constitutes a miscarriage of justice.

image.altText: AFL star midfielder Zak Butters.

items.title: Teen accused of toilet block assault took 'calculated steps', court hears

children.content: Prosecutors say the allegations against a teenage boy accused of sexually assaulting a child in a toilet block are so serious he should face trial in an adult court.

image.altText: A street sign that sayd "Regano Rd", with another reading "Toilet" below it.

items.title: Wine grape grower bakes cupcakes to pay bills as fuel costs bite in 'worst' harvest

children.content: Farmers in the midst of harvest in South Australia are having to get creative to help make ends meet.

image.altText: A fair-skinned woman in a red floral dress holds a cupcake on a glass plate in a kitchen.

items.title: Blood donation eligibility expands for gay and bisexual men, transgender people

children.content: From today, Australian Red Cross Lifeblood will use gender-neutral assessments for blood and platelets donations.

image.altText: a man sits in a reclinable chair with a bandage on his inner elbow and he squeezes a stress ball

items.title: Potato company fined $360,000 after propeller 'severely damaged' employee's arm

children.content: A Southern Mallee Potato company has been convicted and fined $360,000 after a plane propeller struck an employee's arm, leaving him with multiple injuries, including partial finger amputations.

image.altText: A crate of potato's on a farm.

items.title: 500,000 Australians wait for end to 'limbo' as Butler readies NDIS plan

children.content: NDIS Minister Mark Butler must end the uncertainty half a million people have been living in when he announces changes to the nation's disability scheme, mental health advocates say.

articles.title: Government to pay for showers for at-home aged care recipients

children.content: The decision to scrap the plan to charge older Australians $50/hour for showers was made after months of sustained criticism, including warnings that people were forgoing other care to afford the service.

image.altText: Mark Butler standing in a corridor in a suit

articles.title: Closure of specialist centres for children with autism puts spotlight on NDIS funding

children.content: The nationwide closure of specialist centres has put a spotlight on the adequacy of National Disability Insurance Scheme funding for children with "severe, profound, lifelong" autism.

image.altText: A man smiles towards the camera sitting with a young boys in his lap who smiles while looking into the distance.

gallery.altText: Two men in dark suits enter the lower house.

thumbnail.altText: Two men in dark suits enter the lower house.

items.title: Fourth man charged over alleged kidnapping murder of Chris Baghsarian

children.content: The 85-year-old's abduction triggered an 11-day search, with his remains later found near a golf course in Pitt Town, in the city's north-west.

image.altText: Chris Baghsarian looking at the camera the man was mistakenly kidnapped

items.title: Sacked civil servant describes pressure to approve Mandelson as UK ambassador to US

children.content: The former head of the Foreign Office said there was "a very, very strong expectation" that Peter Mandelson "needed to be in post and in America as quickly as possible".

image.altText: A man in a black scarf walks sternly.

items.title: Carpooling an 'effective solution' to fuel prices, so why aren't more doing it?

children.content: Despite fuel prices soaring in March, the number of vehicles travelling along a key carpooling lane in Sydney increased by just 1 per cent. A transport expert says more needs to be done to encourage people to share their commutes.

gallery.altText: A mix of cars and trucks fill four lanes of peak hour traffic on freeway.
A man with grey hair and a beard sitting in the front seat of a vehicle.

thumbnail.altText: A mix of cars and trucks fill four lanes of peak hour traffic on freeway.
A man with grey hair and a beard sitting in the front seat of a vehicle.

items.title: Man jailed for mimicking shooting on bridge used in Bondi attack

children.content: A man who yelled antisemitic profanities while pretending to shoot at people from a pedestrian bridge used by the Bondi Beach terror attack gunmen has been sentenced.

image.altText: Bondi Beach bridge three weeks after terrorist attack.

items.title: Travel quiz: Grab your bags and see if you can beat our quiz

children.content: Which city is hosting the first game of the World Cup? What's the name of Egypt's currency?

image.altText: A few dozen people swim in beautiful blue water giving off steam and mist in front of a picturesque mountain range.

items.title: 'It's been horrible': Your experiences with family estrangement

children.content: Our recent stories about the grief of family estrangement resonated strongly. Here is how some Australians have been affected by it.

gallery.altText: An illustration of a woman sitting on a park bench looking update with her hands on her head

thumbnail.altText: An illustration of a woman sitting on a park bench looking update with her hands on her head

items.title: Government pours $30m into counter-drone technology in wake of Iran war

children.content: The Australian government signs two contracts to develop counter-drone technology, including a laser that can track an object the size of a 10-cent coin travelling at more than 100 km/h.

image.altText: A small grey drone sits on a table with Sypaq banner in the background.

items.title: Boyd says other AFL players are struggling 'under the surface'

children.content: Tom Boyd, former AFL footballer turned mental health advocate, says the high-pressure environment of professional football means many players are struggling with mental health issues "under the surface".

image.altText: Tom Boyd speaks during a tribute to Danny Frawley before an AFL game.

items.title: 'Outrageous mess': About 2,000 AI road safety camera fines withdrawn in WA

children.content: About 2,000 fines valued at $1.1 million issued as a result of AI-assisted road safety cameras in WA have been withdrawn, six months after the devices were introduced.

image.altText: A screenshot of a fine issues for a seatbelt issue

items.title: Legal action considered after Dezi Freeman property entry

children.content: Property owner considers legal action after people enter the Upper Murray property where Dezi Freeman was shot.

image.altText: Dezi Freeman pictured outside a court in Wangaratta.

items.title: Researchers develop surfboard fins with shark-deterrent technology

children.content: A multi-million-dollar project run out of the University of Wollongong is working on fitting shark-deterrent technology into surfboard fins following recent shark attacks.

gallery.altText: Man sanding surfboard in lab, wearing facemask and protective glasses

thumbnail.altText: Man sanding surfboard in lab, wearing facemask and protective glasses

items.title: Residents urged to evacuate New Zealand's capital after floods

children.content: People living in low-lying areas of New Zealand's capital city, Wellington, are urged to evacuate as heavy rain drenches the city.

image.altText: car on a fence.

items.title: 'Pretty invisible': The rise of ADHD medication being prescribed to young women

children.content: Within the sharp increase of ADHD rates in Australia lies a hidden story — more women are being prescribed medication than men. Understanding why is more complicated.

image.altText: A graphic image with silhouettes of men and women's side profiles overlaid with medication and two upwards arrows.

items.title: Paramedics unable to help bleeding mother for almost an hour, inquest hears

children.content: A paramedic tells an inquest she decided to wait for police back-up before attending to a woman who was actively harming herself in a Northern Territory domestic violence shelter.

image.altText: The exterior of the Darwin Local Court.

items.title: Police allege trio used edged weapons, slingshot in 'violent incident'

children.content: A man remains in a critical condition in hospital, and three others have been charged by police, after a "violent" altercation in the remote Northern Territory community of Ngukurr.

image.altText: An aerial photo of houses in a remote community

items.title: El Salvador holds mass trial of alleged gang members for 29,000 murders

children.content: The attorney-general says 486 suspected MS-13 members have faced trial for 47,000 crimes committed between 2012 and 2022.

gallery.altText: several rows of inmates dressed in white sit down inside a prison hall

thumbnail.altText: several rows of inmates dressed in white sit down inside a prison hall
